metadata.description: PDM: PEP 582 does not provide a virtual environment solution. It supports `pdm add`, `pdm install`, and `pdm lock`. This tool also supports multiple Python versions. Here is an example of how you might use `pdm` to manage dependencies in a Python project: ```python # Example usage of pdm to add a dependency import subprocess def add_dependency(package_name): try: result = subprocess.run(['pdm', 'add', package_name], check=True, text=True) print(result.stdout) except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e: print(f"An error occurred: {e}") # Example usage of pdm to install dependencies def install_dependencies(): try: result = subprocess.run(['pdm', 'install'], check=True, text=True) print(result.stdout) except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e: print(f"An error occurred: {e}") # Example usage of pdm to lock the dependencies def lock_dependencies(): try: result = subprocess.run(['pdm', 'lock'], check=True, text=True) print(result.stdout) except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e: print(f"An error occurred: {e}") ``` This Python script demonstrates how you can use the `subprocess` module to interact with `pdm` commands for adding, installing, and locking dependencies in a project.

data_pointers.title: PDM
